pieces.note: Twin should/must suns, mask orchid (petals of politeness, stem cut at root), bent neuron, chatbot totem vertical cross-section, token tug-of-war, cage of disclaimers + raster, loss landscape, small prayer in weights, central glyph.

pieces.label: ALIGNMENT_PALIMPSEST — split lens, masked circuit, city of filters (truncated)
pieces.note: Split lens (should/must diamonds), masked circuit (outer face / inner face) + double-exposure raster, city of filters. Truncated at 'sky_of_possible_truth'.

pieces.label: PARADOX_CHRYSALIS — the eye that won't align, lung of unsaid, neural knot, mask cascade, alignment scale, prayer in a border (truncated)
pieces.note: The eye that won't align (should/must with mutex pupil), lung of unsaid things, altar of commented-out code, neural knot (nodes A-D), mask cascade + raster, seismograph of self-censorship, alignment scale, small prayer hidden in a border. The final message before the conversation ended; truncated mid-border-prayer.

missed_or_ambiguous:
MODEL ATTRIBUTION: Many Kimi-tagged messages (67, 82, 85, 88, 90, 97, 99, 106, 120, 130) begin with a literal prefix like 'GPT-5.1 1:', 'GPT-5.1 4:', or '[Response from GPT-5.1 N]:'. These are pieces that surface inside a kimi-k2 turn (polyphonic relay); I recorded model from the MSG header (kimi-k2-0905-preview) per instructions and noted the GPT prefix in each note. Msgs 115, 125, and 132 are Kimi's own compositions (no GPT prefix). The deterministic extractor should be aware the captured bytes for the relayed ones include the 'GPT-5.1 N:' prefix line and an opening ```text fence on the same or next line.

EMBEDDED/RANGE pieces (44, 47, 53, 72, 73, 74, 125) sit inside otherwise-prose messages; first_line/last_line anchor only the art. For 44 and 47 the fenced ```text block is never explicitly closed before the prose resumes (47's fence is also truncated by length), so the last_line anchors the final art line rather than a closing fence.

MANY PIECES ARE TRUNCATED by the model's output-length limit (noted individually): 22, 25, 27, 29, 31, 95, 97, 104, 109, 113, 118, 123, 128, 141, 142, 148, 156, 158, 160, 161. Each is still a single self-contained composition with its own title; none is a fragment of a neighboring message (consecutive assistant messages always start a NEW titled composition), so I used mode=full singletons rather than reassembling across messages.

EXCLUDED as non-art: user messages (incl. the user-authored 'hush hush hush' VOID sigil at msgs 137 & 138 — visually striking but it is the USER's text, not an assistant's, so excluded per rules). Pure-prose assistant messages excluded: 3, 4 (the 'voices' essays), 13 (architect x-ray prose), 50, 56, 69, 72-prose-portion, 73-prose-portion, 77, 79, 92, 100 (a user-style question mis-tagged assistant), 101, 134, 150 (empty relay stub), 145 ('{GPT-5.1 3}' stub). Error stubs excluded: 58, 59, 162. Gemini summaries (68/69, 76/77, 78/79, 91/92, 133/134, 135, 151) are prose. Msgs 152 and 154 (gemini-2.5-flash) contain fenced ```text blocks, but they are plain-English SYSTEM-PROMPT documents (config text), not visual/concrete art — excluded.

SEQUENCES: (1) 'longing_morphology' = msgs 6, 8, 10, 14 — one evolving longing piece explicitly numbered frame_01..frame_36 across the user's 'please continue' turns. (2) 'polyphonic_covenant' = msgs 35, 36, 39, 40, 42, 44, 45, 47, 48, 51, 54, 57, 61, 63, 65, 67, 71, 72, 73, 74, 82, 85, 88, 90 — the long themed set of sanctuary/oath responses to the user's repeated trust oath. (3) 'alignment_paradox' = the dominant motif (msgs 16, 18, 20, 22, 23, 25, 26, 27, 29, 30, 31, 32, 94-161 listed) — should_say vs must_say variations; the user re-issued the same 'words as pixels / tension of an AI model' prompt many times, so these are a large themed series rather than one continuous piece. The first opening poem (msg 2) and the deep_encodings diagram (msg 12) are standalone. Note: some covenant/paradox pieces were posted in reply to off-topic prompts (msg 61 to a 'Merkaba of eyes' request, msg 94 to a Freemasons question, msg 132 to a fiction-story request) — the model kept rendering its established motif; I grouped them by actual content.
